summary: Gruber reviews the 2020 iPad Pros as a minor spec bump over the already-excellent 2018 models, with virtually unchanged CPU performance and only slightly improved GPU. The standout new features are lidar for AR, an upgraded dual-lens camera system, and improved microphones. He argues the lidar sensor is impressive but only matters to AR developers and enthusiasts right now, while noting it lays groundwork for Apple's AR-first future device. The review serves partly as a prelude to the real product he wants to test: the upcoming Magic Keyboard with trackpad. His bottom line is clear — 2018 iPad Pro owners should skip this upgrade, but new buyers get an excellent device.
key_points: The A12Z chip is essentially unchanged from the A12X in CPU performance, making this a spec bump rather than a speed bump — yet iPad Pro still outperforms the new MacBook Air
Lidar dramatically improves AR accuracy and eliminates the warmup period, but the overwhelming majority of users won't find this compelling enough to upgrade
The iPad Pro lacks Portrait mode despite having similar cameras to iPhone 11, likely because the A12Z's Neural Engine can't match the A13's machine learning capabilities
Apple's whole ARKit-on-iPad effort is laying groundwork for a future AR-first device to launch with developer support from day one
The upcoming Magic Keyboard with trackpad is the real story — it's compatible with 2018 models, giving existing owners reason to hold rather than upgrade
money_quotes: I'm using the phrase 'spec bump' rather than 'speed bump' to describe these new iPad Pros because they don't really seem to be much faster than the 2018 models.

I think an AR revolution is coming, and the whole 'use your iPhone and iPad as ARKit devices' effort on Apple's part — and it's a massive effort — is laying the groundwork for an AR-first device to hit the ground running with developer support from day one.
The truth is I just don't like the Smart Keyboard cover. I don't like typing on it, and I want a trackpad.
These are not new iPad Pros so much as tweaked iPad Pros.
Waiting for rumored future products is a good way to tie yourself in knots and wind up waiting forever.
If you need a new iPad now, these are the best iPads Apple has ever made, and arguably the best portable computers Apple has ever made, period.
The lidar sensor in the new iPad Pro has tremendous accuracy on the Z axis (depth), but not so much on the X and Y axes. It just doesn't project that many dots.
key_insight: The 2020 iPad Pro is a holding-pattern update whose most interesting story is what it foreshadows — Apple's AR ambitions and the upcoming Magic Keyboard — rather than what it delivers today.
